cards.answer_text: When implementing custom filtering logic external to cmdk
cards.explanation: shouldFilter={false} disables cmdk's built-in filtering. Use when: fetch-as-you-type with debounce, server-side search, or custom ranking algorithm. You control what renders in CommandList.

cards.question: How does useHotkeys handle platform modifier differences?

options.A: No platform detection — use 'meta' for both Mac and Windows
options.B: Automatically maps meta to cmd on Mac, ctrl on Windows
options.C: Developer must provide separate key combos per platform

cards.answer_text: Automatically maps meta to cmd on Mac, ctrl on Windows
cards.explanation: react-hotkey detects OS and maps 'meta' to command key on macOS and ctrl on Windows/Linux. useHotkeys('meta+k') works on both platforms without conditional logic.

cards.question: How to add keyboard shortcut hints to CommandItems?

options.A: Not supported — shortcuts displayed in separate component
options.B: Use <CommandShortcut> children inside CommandItem
options.D: Shortcuts are automatically detected from useHotkeys

cards.answer_text: Use <CommandShortcut> children inside CommandItem
cards.explanation: CommandShortcut renders styled keyboard shortcut text inside CommandItem. Visual only — does not register key bindings. Pair with useHotkeys for actual key handling.

cards.question: What happens when a user types a query that matches no commands?

options.B: <CommandEmpty> renders with the unmatched query text

cards.answer_text: <CommandEmpty> renders with the unmatched query text
cards.explanation: CommandEmpty renders when no CommandItem matches current search query. Typically shows 'No results found' or 'No results for {query}'. Only visible when its parent CommandList has no visible CommandItems.

cards.question: How to handle async data fetching in command items?

options.A: cmdk has built-in async support — pass fetch function
options.B: Fetch in useEffect with query dependency, abort previous request, render results as CommandItems
options.C: Fetch all data upfront — async fetching not supported
options.D: Use Web Workers for async command data

cards.answer_text: Fetch in useEffect with query dependency, abort previous request, render results as CommandItems
cards.explanation: Implement async fetch in useEffect. Use query state as deps. Abort previous fetch on new query via AbortController. Map results to CommandItems. Show loading state during fetch.

cards.question: What Babel macro component renders ICU plural forms in JSX?

options.B: <Plural>

cards.answer_text: <Plural>
cards.explanation: <Plural> is Lingui's ICU plural component. It handles count-based text selection with one/other (and few/many for Slavic languages).

cards.question: Lingui compiles messages at build time. What is the primary benefit over runtime i18n?

options.A: Smaller bundle — no runtime parser needed
options.B: Faster locale switching

cards.answer_text: Smaller bundle — no runtime parser needed
cards.explanation: Babel macros remove ICU parser from bundle. Application ships only compiled messages. Runtime approaches (react-i18next) include parser in bundle.

cards.question: What CLI flag generates typed message catalogs for autocomplete?

options.B: --typed

cards.answer_text: --typescript
cards.explanation: 'lingui compile --typescript' generates .d.ts type declaration files alongside compiled catalogs, enabling autocomplete for message IDs.

cards.question: Which method activates a locale after loading its catalog?

options.B: i18n.activate(locale)

cards.answer_text: i18n.activate(locale)
cards.explanation: 'i18n.load(locale, messages)' loads catalog. 'i18n.activate(locale)' sets active locale and triggers re-render via I18nProvider.

cards.question: ICU message: '{name} uploaded {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}}' What does # represent?

options.B: The message ID
options.C: A hash placeholder for future text

cards.answer_text: The count value
cards.explanation: # in ICU plural forms is replaced with the numeric value of the count argument. '{count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}}' with count=1 renders '1 file'.

cards.question: When does msg macro fail to extract a message?

options.B: When argument is a dynamic variable, not a template literal — msg(someVar) cannot be extracted
options.D: When message is longer than 100 characters

cards.answer_text: When argument is a dynamic variable, not a template literal — msg(someVar) cannot be extracted
cards.explanation: Macros operate on AST at compile time. Static template literal msg`Hello ${name}` is analyzable. Dynamic variable msg(someVar) cannot be extracted. Always use template literals.

cards.question: What Lingui component renders gender-based text selection?

options.B: <Select>

cards.answer_text: <Select>
cards.explanation: <Select value={gender} male="He" female="She" other="They" /> handles gender-based text. ICU select format supports arbitrary category values.

cards.question: How to lazy-load Lingui catalogs for code-split bundles?

options.A: Load all catalogs eagerly — lazy loading not supported
options.B: Dynamic import of catalog module: await import('./locales/' + locale + '/messages')

cards.answer_text: Dynamic import of catalog module: await import('./locales/' + locale + '/messages')
cards.explanation: Dynamic import keeps locale catalogs in separate chunks. Only active locale's catalog loaded. 'i18n.load(locale, messages)' after import.

cards.question: Lingui vs react-i18next: when is react-i18next the better choice?

options.B: When translation keys are dynamic and change without deployments (CMS-driven content)
options.C: When app targets only one locale

cards.answer_text: When translation keys are dynamic and change without deployments (CMS-driven content)
cards.explanation: Runtime key resolution allows dynamic translation keys from CMS. Lingui requires rebuild for new messages. For CMS-driven multilingual apps, react-i18next flexibility wins.

cards.question: What happens if a Lingui message ID is missing from the active catalog?

options.B: Runtime error thrown
options.C: Source message string used as fallback

cards.answer_text: Source message string used as fallback
cards.explanation: Lingui embeds source message strings as fallback. Missing translation renders the source code message. No blank UI or runtime error — graceful degradation.

cards.question: What Liveblocks hook reads shared storage state reactively?

options.B: useMyPresence

cards.answer_text: useStorage
cards.explanation: useStorage reads Live structures (LiveList, LiveObject, LiveMap) and re-renders component when any observed property changes.

cards.question: What is the difference between Storage and Presence?

options.A: Storage persists across sessions; presence is transient (cursors, selection)
options.B: Storage is client-only; presence syncs to server
options.C: Storage is read-only; presence is writable

cards.answer_text: Storage persists across sessions; presence is transient (cursors, selection)
cards.explanation: Storage holds durable shared state (documents, blocks). Presence holds ephemeral state (cursor position, focus) not persisted after disconnect.

cards.question: How does Liveblocks undo/redo handle changes from other users?

options.A: Undoing Alice's change removes it for everyone
options.B: History is local — each client has own undo stack; undo reverses client's own mutations on shared storage
options.C: Undo only affects local presence, not storage

cards.answer_text: History is local — each client has own undo stack; undo reverses client's own mutations on shared storage
cards.explanation: Each client has independent undo stack. Undoing Alice's own mutation reverts that change from shared storage. Bob sees the block disappear. No per-user isolated history.

cards.question: What data structure stores an ordered list of objects in Liveblocks storage?

options.B: LiveList

cards.answer_text: LiveList
cards.explanation: LiveList is ordered sequence supporting insert, move, delete, and push operations. Children are LiveObject or other Live structures.

cards.question: Presence data includes cursor coordinates. How often should presence be sent?

options.B: Throttled to ~30fps or 33ms intervals for reasonable bandwidth

cards.answer_text: Throttled to ~30fps or 33ms intervals for reasonable bandwidth
cards.explanation: Sending every pointer-move event generates excessive WebSocket messages. Throttle to ~30fps. For 100+ concurrent users, consider throttling further or limiting to visible users.

cards.question: Which hook provides threads for comment annotations?

options.B: useThreads

cards.answer_text: useThreads
cards.explanation: useThreads returns all threads in current room. useCreateThread creates new thread with body content and metadata (position, resolved status).

cards.question: Two clients simultaneously rename same block to different names. What happens?

options.B: Last-write-wins — CRDT converges to single name based on deterministic ordering
options.C: First write wins — second rename rejected
options.D: Block splits into two blocks with different names

cards.answer_text: Last-write-wins — CRDT converges to single name based on deterministic ordering
cards.explanation: Liveblocks CRDT uses last-write-wins for scalar values. Concurrent operations ordered by vector clock converge deterministically. Both clients see same final state.

cards.question: What makes a useMutation call undoable as a single step?

options.A: Each mutation call is automatically one undoable step
options.B: Must call history.batch() to group operations
options.D: Mutations are not undoable by default

cards.answer_text: Each mutation call is automatically one undoable step
cards.explanation: Each useMutation invocation is one undoable history entry. Multiple storage.set() calls inside same mutation are grouped as one undo step.

cards.question: Why must room state be persisted to external database periodically?

options.A: Liveblocks does not store data durably — in-memory CRDT lost when room becomes idle
options.B: Liveblocks has no storage capability
options.C: Database persistence is optional — Liveblocks handles durability

cards.answer_text: Liveblocks does not store data durably — in-memory CRDT lost when room becomes idle
cards.explanation: Liveblocks holds CRDT state in memory for active rooms. State lost when all clients disconnect or room idle. Use REST API to read state and persist to database on save events.

cards.question: React 19 Suspense with RoomProvider: what does Suspense wait for?

options.B: Room connection establishes and initial storage loads
options.C: All presence data arrives from other clients

cards.answer_text: Room connection establishes and initial storage loads
cards.explanation: useSuspenseStorage triggers Suspense boundary during room connection and initial storage synchronization. Component renders only after storage is available.

cards.question: What Clerk component conditionally renders children when user is signed in?

options.B: <SignedIn>

cards.answer_text: <SignedIn>
cards.explanation: SignedIn renders children only when user session is active. SignedOut renders children when no session. Pair them for auth-gated layout.

cards.question: What hook provides organization role and permissions?

options.B: useAuth

cards.answer_text: useAuth
cards.explanation: useAuth returns orgRole, orgId, orgPermissions, sessionId, getToken. useUser returns user profile data (name, email, image).

cards.question: How to get a session token for backend API authentication?

options.B: Call user.getToken() or useAuth().getToken()

cards.answer_text: Call user.getToken() or useAuth().getToken()
cards.explanation: getToken() returns a short-lived JWT signed by Clerk. Send as Authorization: Bearer header to backend. Supports token templates for custom claims.

cards.question: What Protect permission attribute checks specific action-level access?

options.B: permission

cards.answer_text: permission
cards.explanation: Protect accepts both role (org-level) and permission (action-level). 'org:sys:memberships:manage' is a permission. 'org:admin' is a role.

cards.question: What is the correct server-side helper to verify auth in a React 19 Server Component?

options.B: auth() from @clerk/nextjs/server

cards.answer_text: auth() from @clerk/nextjs/server
cards.explanation: auth() from @clerk/nextjs/server returns userId, orgId, sessionClaims for the current request. Works in Server Components and Route Handlers.

cards.question: What happens when social auth strategy is disabled in Clerk Dashboard?

options.A: App must redeploy to reflect change
options.B: Clerk UI components automatically hide disabled strategy — no code change
options.D: Disabled strategy remains visible but returns 403

cards.answer_text: Clerk UI components automatically hide disabled strategy — no code change
cards.explanation: Clerk UI components fetch enabled strategies from Clerk API at runtime. Disabling Google SSO in dashboard immediately hides Google button. Zero code changes.

cards.question: What event type indicates a new user signed up via webhook?

options.B: user.created

cards.answer_text: user.created
cards.explanation: Clerk webhook event 'user.created' fires on new user registration. Payload includes user data (id, email, name, image). Use to sync user to database.

cards.question: What middleware configuration sets routes accessible without authentication?

options.B: publicRoutes

cards.answer_text: publicRoutes
cards.explanation: publicRoutes in clerkMiddleware config defines routes accessible without auth (landing page, sign-in, sign-up, webhook endpoints). All other routes protected by default.

cards.question: How to switch active organization programmatically?

options.B: Call setActive({ organization: orgId }) from useOrganizationList

cards.answer_text: Call setActive({ organization: orgId }) from useOrganizationList
cards.explanation: setActive from useOrganizationList switches current organization. Session token is re-issued with new org claims. All Protect checks re-evaluate against new org role.

cards.question: What TypeScript import provides types for UserResource and SessionResource?

options.B: @clerk/types

cards.answer_text: @clerk/types
cards.explanation: @clerk/types package exports UserResource, SessionResource, OrganizationResource, and other TypeScript interfaces for Clerk data models.

cards.question: What does asChild prop do in Radix primitives?

options.A: Renders the primitive as a child of parent component
options.B: Delegates rendering to child element — child receives Radix props and event handlers
options.C: Renders primitive as HTML child element

cards.answer_text: Delegates rendering to child element — child receives Radix props and event handlers
cards.explanation: asChild merges Radix' behavior (events, ARIA attributes, refs) onto the child element. Child must be a valid React element that accepts forwarded props.

cards.question: Which Radix primitive provides focus trapping for modals?

options.B: <FocusScope>

cards.answer_text: <FocusScope>
cards.explanation: FocusScope handles focus containment. Used internally by Dialog. Supports loop (cycle through focusable elements) and trapped (prevent focus leaving) modes.

cards.question: How to close a DropdownMenu item on selection?

options.B: onSelect callback fires — menu closes automatically by default
options.D: Menu does not close on selection by default

cards.answer_text: onSelect callback fires — menu closes automatically by default
cards.explanation: DropdownMenu.Item.onSelect fires on click/Enter. Menu closes automatically after onSelect handler runs. Prevent default to keep menu open.

cards.question: What is the purpose of Radix's unstyled approach?

options.A: Reduce bundle size by excluding CSS
options.B: Give developers full control over styling while providing production-grade accessibility

cards.answer_text: Give developers full control over styling while providing production-grade accessibility
cards.explanation: Unstyled means Radix provides behavior (keyboard nav, focus, ARIA) without assumptions about appearance. Developers style freely while getting battle-tested accessibility.

cards.question: Tabs component uses which keyboard navigation model?

options.A: Tab to switch tabs — tabindex=0 for all
options.B: Roving tabindex — arrow keys navigate, Tab moves to active tab panel content

cards.answer_text: Roving tabindex — arrow keys navigate, Tab moves to active tab panel content
cards.explanation: Tabs uses roving tabindex: only active tab is in tab order. Arrow keys move focus between tabs. Tab key moves focus from active tab into tab panel content.

cards.question: What data attribute does Radix set on open overlay elements?

options.B: data-visibile

cards.answer_text: data-[state=open]
cards.explanation: Radix uses data-[state=open] and data-[state=closed] on elements that toggle visibility. CSS targets these for transitions: '.overlay[data-state=open] { opacity: 1 }'

cards.question: When would you NOT use Radix and build custom component instead?

options.B: When interaction is extremely simple with no complex keyboard or a11y requirements
options.D: When app is large with 50+ components

cards.answer_text: When interaction is extremely simple with no complex keyboard or a11y requirements
cards.explanation: Simple toggle buttons or static lists do not need Radix overhead. Radix valuable for complex interactive components: modals, menus, selects, comboboxes.

cards.question: Select component uses which ARIA pattern?

options.B: listbox

cards.answer_text: listbox
cards.explanation: Radix Select implements ARIA listbox pattern: role=listbox on content, role=option on items, aria-selected for current value, aria-activedescendant for focus.

cards.question: How to compose Radix Popover with Framer Motion animations?

options.B: Wrap Popover.Content with AnimatePresence and use data-[state=open] for variants

cards.answer_text: Wrap Popover.Content with AnimatePresence and use data-[state=open] for variants
cards.explanation: Popover.Content inside AnimatePresence with key based on open state. Variants use data-[state=open] and data-[state=closed] for enter/exit animations.

cards.question: Tooltip delayDuration controls what?

options.A: How long tooltip stays visible after mouse leaves
options.B: Delay before tooltip appears on hover (default 700ms)

cards.answer_text: Delay before tooltip appears on hover (default 700ms)
cards.explanation: delayDuration (ms) on Tooltip.Provider sets hover-to-show delay. Default 700ms. Also supports skipDelayDuration for rapid re-triggering.

cards.question: What is the difference between staleTime and gcTime (formerly cacheTime)?

options.B: staleTime = how long data is considered fresh (no refetch). gcTime = how long unused data stays in cache before garbage collection
options.C: staleTime = cache duration. gcTime = retry timeout
options.D: staleTime = server cache. gcTime = client cache

cards.answer_text: staleTime = how long data is considered fresh (no refetch). gcTime = how long unused data stays in cache before garbage collection
cards.explanation: staleTime controls refetch behavior (fresh data does not refetch). gcTime controls cache eviction (data kept in cache after last observer unmounts). Default: staleTime=0, gcTime=5min.

cards.question: What does useQuery return when cache has data but a background refetch is in progress?

options.B: isLoading: false, isFetching: true, data: cached data
options.D: isLoading: true, isFetching: true, data: undefined

cards.answer_text: isLoading: false, isFetching: true, data: cached data
cards.explanation: isLoading = no cached data AND fetch in progress (first load). isFetching = any fetch in progress. Background refetch shows cached data with isFetching=true.

cards.question: What TanStack Query v5 option replaces keepPreviousData: true for pagination?

options.B: placeholderData: keepPreviousData

cards.answer_text: placeholderData: keepPreviousData
cards.explanation: v5 renamed keepPreviousData to placeholderData: keepPreviousData. Shows previous page data while next page loads, avoiding loading spinner flash.

cards.question: How to prevent a query from fetching until a condition is met?

options.A: Set enabled: condition in query options
options.B: Wrap query in if statement

cards.answer_text: Set enabled: condition in query options
cards.explanation: enabled option gates query execution. enabled: false means query never runs. enabled: !!userId means query runs when userId is truthy. Used for dependent queries.

cards.question: Optimistic update rollback: where to store snapshot of previous data?

options.B: Return from onMutate — passed as context to onError
options.D: Snapshot not needed — TanStack Query auto-rolls back

cards.answer_text: Return from onMutate — passed as context to onError
cards.explanation: onMutate returns context object. If mutation fails, onError receives context as third argument. Use context.previous to restore cached data via setQueryData.

cards.question: What hook provides incremental data loading with cursor-based pagination?

options.B: useInfiniteQuery

cards.answer_text: useInfiniteQuery
cards.explanation: useInfiniteQuery supports cursor-based pagination. getNextPageParam returns next cursor from last page response. fetchNextPage loads next page, appending to data.pages array.

cards.question: What is the difference between queryClient.invalidateQueries and queryClient.refetchQueries?

options.B: invalidateQueries marks queries as stale (refetch on next render). refetchQueries triggers immediate refetch regardless of stale state
options.C: invalidateQueries triggers immediate refetch. refetchQueries marks stale

cards.answer_text: invalidateQueries marks queries as stale (refetch on next render). refetchQueries triggers immediate refetch regardless of stale state
cards.explanation: invalidateQueries sets stale time to 0 — queries refetch on next observer mount or window focus. refetchQueries forces immediate refetch of matching queries regardless of staleTime.

cards.question: React 19: which query hook integrates with Suspense boundaries?

options.B: useSuspenseQuery
options.C: useSuspenseQuery replaces useQuery for Suspense — throws promise to Suspense boundary

cards.answer_text: useSuspenseQuery replaces useQuery for Suspense — throws promise to Suspense boundary
cards.explanation: useSuspenseQuery throws promise during fetch, caught by parent Suspense boundary. Eliminates isLoading check — component renders only with data.

cards.question: How to skip query execution in a Suspense-enabled component without enabling/disabling?

options.B: Use skipToken as queryFn when condition not met

cards.answer_text: Use skipToken as queryFn when condition not met
cards.explanation: skipToken is special sentinel value. When queryFn equals skipToken, query does not execute. Use for conditional queries with Suspense: queryFn: userId ? fetchUser : skipToken.

cards.question: useMutation.onSuccess and queryClient.invalidateQueries: what is the common pattern?

options.A: Invalidate is automatic — no manual call needed
options.B: After mutation succeeds, invalidate related queries so they refetch fresh data
options.C: Invalidate removes queries from cache permanently
options.D: onSuccess is optional — invalidation happens in onSettled only

cards.answer_text: After mutation succeeds, invalidate related queries so they refetch fresh data
cards.explanation: Standard pattern: mutation succeeds → invalidate queries that include mutated data → components subscribed to those queries refetch. Example: create user → invalidate ['users'].
